scenario: Prioritize features by how much they help an OpenClaw orchestrator agent manage hundreds of Codex sessions and other agent sessions with the least context, least rereading, and safest next-action selection.
pass_criteria: A feature scores highest when it gives the orchestrator more accurate session understanding with fewer tokens.
The highest-scoring work improves session metadata, project/status/priority routing, closeout quality, archive/fork handling, or bounded retrieval.
Retrieval improvements favor cited, source-prefixed refs and honest omitted markers over larger raw expansions.
Cockpit and operating-picture features improve deterministic prioritization with source coverage, confidence, freshness, and reason codes.
Automation hooks improve metadata and closeout quality without performing live control or GUI mutation.
Roadmap decisions record why lower-scoring visual, adapter, or desktop features wait behind orchestrator leverage work.
fail_criteria: A feature is prioritized mainly because it is visually impressive while leaving the orchestrator with more context to read.
A feature requires broad raw transcript reads to manage sessions.
A feature blurs read-only recall with live control or GUI mutation.
A feature creates public evidence from raw sessions, DBs, screenshots, prompts, or secrets.
Roadmap scoring cannot explain why one feature outranks another.
known_gaps: Weights are a v1 product rubric and should be recalibrated after local OpenClaw gateway dogfood and retrieval evals produce real score movement.
P1 operating-picture adapters for Notion, support-control, Company Brain, Stripe, dashboard/export, and model summarization still need child issues after P0 cards prove clean.
Hybrid retrieval, hook agents, archive/fork controls, Mac UI, and sanitizer repair execution still need child issues before implementation.
next_action: Use the weighted examples to rank the next post-#278 child issue before adding P1 business adapters or broader desktop work.
proof_boundary: Scored as an advisory public-safe beta roadmap-prioritization review through issue #278. It guides issue order only and does not prove beta readiness, live control safety, adapter parity, P1 business adapters, customer readiness, or release approval.
highest_priority_examples: thread metadata and closeout schema for project, status, priority, owner, blocker, next action, and source refs
closeout or hook agents that tag finished threads and plans with public-safe metadata
session map that lets the orchestrator sort, archive, fork, and resume sessions without raw transcript reads
autonomy cockpit inbox that ranks recent sessions into public-safe attention cards without wildcard transcript queries
Eva operating picture digest that joins LCO/Codex, normalized public-safe GitHub items, and explicit PLAN_STATE pins while marking missing P1 sources not_configured
LCM-style grep, describe, and expand-query workflow with citations and bounded evidence bundles
hybrid search using BM25, vector retrieval, query expansion, and reranking when fixture evals prove it improves top-k relevance
session sanitizer addon that scans indexed sessions for secret-like strings and produces redacted dry-run repair tasks
simple local Mac search UI after the CLI, MCP, and OpenClaw gateway surfaces prove the underlying recall loop
